extra.message: SQL injection via 'id' parameter: User input from params['id'] is directly concatenated into SQL SELECT query without parameterization. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL code (e.g., '2 OR 1=1') to bypass query logic and extract sensitive data.

SQL injection via 'comment' parameter: User input from params['comment'] is directly interpolated into INSERT statement using string formatting without parameterization. An attacker can inject SQL code (e.g., 'test'; DROP TABLE users; --) to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

SQL injection via 'password' parameter in login query: While the username field is sanitized with regex, the password field is directly concatenated into the SQL query without any sanitization or parameterization. An attacker can inject SQL code (e.g., password=' OR '1'='1) to bypass authentication.

Path traversal vulnerability: User input from params['path'] is passed to open() with os.path.abspath() but without restricting the base directory. An attacker can use traversal sequences (e.g., '../../../../etc/passwd') to read arbitrary files on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: OS command injection via 'domain' parameter: User input from params['domain'] is directly concatenated into an nslookup command executed with shell=True. An attacker can inject shell metacharacters (e.g., '8.8.8.8; rm -rf /') to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: Remote code execution via 'include' parameter: User input from params['include'] is used to fetch a file (local or remote) which is then executed using exec(). An attacker can supply a malicious Python script URL or local path to execute arbitrary Python code with application privileges.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: XPath injection via 'name' parameter: User input from params['name'] is directly interpolated into an XPath expression without sanitization. An attacker can inject XPath syntax (e.g., '//user[name/text()="' or 1=1 or ''=""] to extract unauthorized data.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions

extra.message: Code injection / JSONP callback injection via 'callback' parameter: User input from params['callback'] is directly interpolated into JavaScript output without sanitization. An attacker can inject malicious JavaScript code through the callback parameter.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: XXE (XML External Entity) vulnerability: User input from params['xml'] is parsed using lxml with load_dtd=True and resolve_entities=True. This allows an attacker to define malicious DTD entities to read files, perform SSRF, or cause DoS attacks.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Open redirect vulnerability via 'redir' parameter: User input from params['redir'] is directly inserted into an HTTP refresh meta tag without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external URLs for phishing or malware distribution.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: Unsafe deserialization via pickle.loads(): User input from params['object'] is deserialized directly without validation. An attacker can craft malicious pickled objects to achieve remote code execution.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via 'path' parameter: When the path parameter contains a URL (checked with '://' substring), urllib.request.urlopen() is called with user-controlled input, allowing an attacker to make requests to internal services or arbitrary URLs.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via 'include' parameter: When the include parameter contains a URL, urllib.request.urlopen() is called with user-controlled input, allowing an attacker to fetch arbitrary remote content and execute it as Python code.
